reasoning: The informal statement says: if X is infinite, then X with the finite complement (cofinite) topology is connected. The Lean signature uses `[Infinite X]` to encode "X is infinite", `CofiniteTopology X` (defined in `Mathlib/Topology/Constructions.lean` as the type synonym carrying the topology whose open sets are ∅ and sets with finite complement) to encode "finite complement topology", and `IsConnected (Set.univ : Set (CofiniteTopology X))` to encode "the whole space is connected" (nonempty + preconnected). Asserting connectedness of `univ` in the ambient topological space is the standard Mathlib idiom equivalent to `ConnectedSpace (CofiniteTopology X)`. No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the conclusion matches exactly. Hence the formalization is strictly faithful.
